Question: ALTER TABLE marc_subfield_structure MODIFY COLUMN authorised_value varchar(10) DEFAULT NULL, MODIFY COLUMN authtypecode varchar(10) DEFAULT NULL;
What was the problem?: The columns authorised_value and authtypecode were defined as varchar(10), which was too short and could truncate valid values.
Answer: ALTER TABLE marc_subfield_structure MODIFY COLUMN authorised_value varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL, MODIFY COLUMN authtypecode varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL;

Question: CREATE TABLE execution_logs (enc_type varchar(128) NOT NULL, exec_id bigint NOT NULL, attempt_id int NOT NULL, name varchar(640) NOT NULL, start_byte bigint DEFAULT 0, end_byte bigint DEFAULT 0, length bigint DEFAULT 0, upload_time bigint DEFAULT 0) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8;
What was the problem?: The execution_logs table was created without a primary key, allowing duplicate rows with the same logical identity.
How would you fix it?: Add a composite PRIMARY KEY on enc_type, exec_id, attempt_id, and name.
Answer: CREATE TABLE execution_logs (enc_type varchar(128) NOT NULL, exec_id bigint NOT NULL, attempt_id int NOT NULL, name varchar(640) NOT NULL, start_byte bigint DEFAULT 0, end_byte bigint DEFAULT 0, length bigint DEFAULT 0, upload_time bigint DEFAULT 0, PRIMARY KEY (enc_type, exec_id, attempt_id, name)) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8;
